value.observation: SEBI settlement case regarding unlisted CRPS distribution - Company paid Rs 14.4 lakh settlement amount for violating private placement norms by down-selling CRPSs to more than 200 investors within 6 months of allotment
FIR lodged against Tata Capital officials in alleged loan fraud case involving forged documents and signatures for mortgaging property without owner's consent
The company has faced regulatory scrutiny over compliance with private placement norms, requiring settlement with SEBI
value.impact: Indicates gaps in regulatory compliance processes though financial impact was limited
value.mitigation: Company has taken corrective measures through settlement

value.observation: Recent allegations of fraud by officials raises concerns about internal controls and governance
value.impact: Could affect reputation and trigger increased regulatory oversight

material_adverse_observations_web_media_.notes: While there are some adverse observations, they appear to be specific incidents rather than systemic issues. Company has generally maintained stable regulatory compliance record.

external_credit_rating_remarks.remarks: CRISIL: AAA/Stable reaffirmed in September 2024. CARE: AAA/Stable reaffirmed in April 2026. ICRA: AAA/Stable for subordinated debt and AA+/Stable for perpetual debt reaffirmed in 2025.

external_credit_rating_trend_remark.remarks: There has been no change in rating since the past 3 years. All three agencies CRISIL, CARE, and ICRA have maintained AAA/Stable for long-term instruments.

ram_rating_movement_compliance.value: No movement — C2/S2 maintained. No change in rating since past 3 years.

ram_rating_remarks.remarks: RAM Rating C2/S2 found in multiple reports for FY25 and FY26.

ram_rating_movement_remarks.remarks: Consistent C2/S2 rating found across FY23, FY24, and FY25 reports.

result.lcr_movement_justification: LCR increased significantly from 105% in FY 2024 to 215.67% in FY 2025, indicating a substantial improvement in the company's liquidity position. This rise suggests enhanced ability to meet short-term obligations and withstand potential liquidity stress.
result.borrowings_remarks: Borrowings have shown an increasing trend, growing from Rs 53,621.10 crore in FY 2023 to Rs 62,309.61 crore in FY 2025. The year-on-year growth rate from FY 2024 to FY 2025 was 13.26%.

result.nim_justification: NIM has shown a steady improvement from 3.84% in FY 2023 to 3.94% in FY 2025, indicating a positive trend in the company's core lending profitability. The increase of 10 basis points over two years suggests gradual enhancement in spread management.
result.yield_justification: Portfolio yield increased from 10.99% in FY 2023 to 11.18% in FY 2024, but then decreased to 10.76% in FY 2025. The overall trend shows a slight decline of 23 basis points from FY 2023 to FY 2025, indicating some pressure on yields.
result.roa_justification: ROA has shown significant improvement, increasing from 1.59% in FY 2023 to 2.52% in FY 2025. This represents a substantial increase of 93 basis points over two years, indicating enhanced profitability and asset utilization.
result.interest_income_justification: Interest income has shown consistent growth, increasing from ₹ 6,445.94 crore in FY 2023 to ₹ 7,605.54 crore in FY 2025. This represents a total increase of ₹ 1,159.60 crore over two years, indicating strong growth in the company's core lending business.
result.total_income_justification: Total income has grown consistently from ₹ 6,492.39 crore in FY 2023 to ₹ 7,661.22 crore in FY 2025. This represents an increase of ₹ 1,168.83 crore over two years, showing strong overall revenue growth.
result.exceptional_income_justification: No extraordinary income was reported for FY 2023, FY 2024, or FY 2025, indicating that the company's income growth was driven by core business activities.
result.opex_justification: Operating expenses have increased from ₹ 474.62 crore in FY 2023 to ₹ 701.94 crore in FY 2025. This represents an increase of ₹ 227.32 crore over two years, indicating rising operational costs.
result.pat_justification: Profit After Tax (PAT) has shown significant growth, increasing from ₹ 1,056.27 crore in FY 2023 to ₹ 1,948.95 crore in FY 2025. This represents a substantial increase of ₹ 892.68 crore over two years, indicating strong improvement in overall profitability.
result.cost_income_justification: The Cost to Income Ratio has increased from 7.31% in FY 2023 to 9.16% in FY 2025. This represents an increase of 1.85 percentage points over two years, indicating a slight deterioration in operational efficiency.
result.credit_cost_justification: Credit cost has significantly improved, decreasing from 1.18% in FY 2023 to -0.22% in FY 2025. This represents a substantial improvement of 140 basis points over two years, indicating better asset quality and lower provisioning requirements.

result.gnpa_nnpa_writeoff_ratio_movement_justif: GNPA increased from 1.5% in FY 2024 to 1.9% in FY 2025, while NNPA increased from 0.4% to 0.8% over the same period. This indicates a deterioration in asset quality. The increase of 0.4 percentage points in both GNPA and NNPA suggests a consistent rise in non-performing assets. Without write-off data, the full impact on asset quality cannot be assessed.

result.remarks: PCR-Stage 3 increased from 28.77% in FY2023 to 37.35% in FY2024, but decreased slightly to 36.05% in FY2025. Overall trend is improving.

result.equity_justification: Share capital increased from ₹169 crore in FY 2023 to ₹260 crore in FY 2024, representing a 53.8% year-over-year growth. It remained stable at ₹260 crore in FY 2025.
result.tangible_networth_justification: Tangible Net Worth grew from ₹10,790 crore in FY 2023 to ₹14,779 crore in FY 2024, and further to ₹16,628 crore in FY 2025. The year-over-year growth from FY 2024 to FY 2025 was 12.5%. This growth was likely driven by retained earnings and possibly capital infusion.
result.net_owned_funds_justification: Net Owned Funds increased from ₹9,471 crore in FY 2023 to ₹13,532 crore in FY 2024, and further to ₹15,573 crore in FY 2025. The year-over-year growth from FY 2024 to FY 2025 was 15.1%. This growth closely aligns with the Tangible Net Worth growth.
result.gearing_justification: The leverage ratio (TOL/TNW) decreased from 4.97 in FY 2023 to 3.72 in FY 2024, and then slightly increased to 3.75 in FY 2025. The year-over-year change from FY 2024 to FY 2025 was a 0.8% increase. The overall trend shows improved leverage position over the three-year period.
result.crar_justification: CRAR improved from 24.43% in FY 2023 to 29.26% in FY 2024, and further to 29.38% in FY 2025. The year-over-year growth from FY 2024 to FY 2025 was 0.4%. The CRAR remains well above the regulatory threshold, indicating a strong capital position.
result.crar_tier1_justification: CRAR (Tier 1) improved from 22.40% in FY 2023 to 27.90% in FY 2024, and further to 28.39% in FY 2025. The year-over-year growth from FY 2024 to FY 2025 was 1.8%. The Tier 1 CRAR remains significantly above the threshold, indicating a strong core capital position.

result.prudential_rating_AAA_and_upper_layer_co: CRISIL AAA/Stable rating for Unsecured NCDs and Bank Facilities. ICRA AAA/Stable and IND AAA/Stable for Unsecured NCDs. Company classified as Middle Layer NBFC (NBFC-ML) as per RBI Scale Based Regulations.
result.nbfc_registered_compilance: Registered with the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) as a Systemically Important Non-Deposit Accepting Core Investment Company (CIC)
result.track_record_compilance: 32nd Annual Report presented, indicating at least 32 years of operation. Consolidated Return on Assets (RoA) for FY 2022-23 was 2.9% and Return on Equity (RoE) was 23%.

Financial Performance.value: Tata Capital Limited reported strong financial performance in FY 2022-23. Total income grew 32% to Rs 13,637 crore. Net profit increased 80% to Rs 2,975 crore, including some one-time gains. Excluding one-time gains, net profit grew 46% to Rs 2,414 crore. Return on Equity improved from 15.6% to 23% (17.2% excluding one-time gains). The loan book expanded 28% to Rs 120,940 crore, with 80% in retail and SME segments. Asset quality improved, with GNPA decreasing from 1.9% to 1.7% and NNPA from 0.6% to 0.4%.
Financial Performance.notes: Data extracted from consolidated financial results and management commentary in annual report
